_note: Guided tour, organized as stages -> stops. Each stage: name, badge (status pill; 'Operating now' renders green), stops[]. Each stop: title, body (blank line = paragraph), images[] (site photos use /images/... ; map aerials + CABN photos use media/...), layers[] (slugs shown; everything else hidden), fit ('all' = whole property, else fits the shown layers), optional cta. Edit freely.

stops.title: The Land
stops.body:
393 acres at the gateway to Wells Gray Provincial Park, near Clearwater, BC — lake, creek, forest and open meadow.

This is a walk through what's already built and operating here, and the village planned to grow on the land. Use the arrows to move through it, or close the tour to explore the layers yourself.

stops.title: Built & Operating
stops.body:
Five seasons in. Full-service and power/water RV sites, rental cabins and a geodesic glamping dome, a 120-person event gazebo with a commercial kitchen, a cedar sauna and cold plunge, shower houses, a private swimming lake and a disc-golf course.

This is the working prototype — proof the land draws people before the village is built on top of it.

stops.title: Maker Space
stops.body:
A community workshop — 3D printers, CNC, laser, woodworking — and the engine of the whole build: it fabricates and finishes the village's cabins and tiny homes.

Infrastructure: wired off the existing electrical trunk that already runs to this corner of the property — a short feed, no new service needed.

stops.title: The Cabins
stops.body:
Small prefab cabins woven into the forest, sited to plug straight into infrastructure that's already here — power, water, septic and Starlink internet all run within a few metres, so each cabin needs only short service spurs (shown on the map).

The plan uses CABN (cabn.co), a BC-interior builder of net-zero, solar-ready cabins — a natural fit for an off-grid-capable village. Models range from the HUTT studio (from ~$39,500, arrives assembled with an in-roof solar array) to the MOR.II (540 sqft, 1-bed/1-bath). CABN ships the high-performance timber shell; the village's own maker space finishes the interiors, keeping the labour, skills and money local.

(Cabin imagery: CABN model photos.)

stops.title: Festival Grounds
stops.body:
Light-touch by design. The work here is just: clear the meadow, cut and clear the trail network, and add a new shower house and grouped portapotties — enough to run a 500-to-5,000-person, leave-no-trace festival with no permanent build.

The timber cleared from the grounds and trails doesn't leave the property — it's milled on site and stockpiled as building material for the later-phase cabins and structures.

stops.title: Maker Village
stops.body:
A cluster of prefab cabins for the people who build the village — housing paired with shared maker infrastructure, built on-site to a repeatable template that can be cloned at other village nodes.

Infrastructure: full power, water, septic and Wi-Fi run to the cluster.

stops.title: RV & Tiny-Home Sites
stops.body:
A tidy expansion of the campground: pull-through RV pads and tiny-home sites.

Infrastructure: upgraded 50-amp power, water and septic, tying into the services already trunked through this area (a new transformer and septic field serve the cluster).

stops.title: Boutique Hotel
stops.body:
A small boutique lodge near the entrance — rooms for visitors and prospective members who aren't camping.

Infrastructure: a low building footprint tied into existing power, water and septic by short spurs (the mains run right past it).

stops.title: Land for Sale
stops.body:
A handful of large acreage lots offered for sale — the capital that funds the rest of the build.

Two layouts are drawn (you can toggle them in the layers panel): uniform ~10-acre lots, or a mix of 10 / 5 / 2.5-acre lots.

stops.title: Artist Village
stops.body: A quiet cabin community on the forested slope above the lake — studios, residencies and creative work, with art treated as infrastructure. Four cabin types — small, medium, hillside, and pale hempcrete cliffside cabins — linked by walking paths.

stops.title: Wellness Village & The Commons
stops.body: The heart of the residential vision: clusters of timber cabins — "pods" around shared greens — spread across the old golf course, every path converging on The Commons (café & kitchen, maker space, co-working, movement + wellness studios), so the whole village meets in one place.

stops.title: Farm & Lakeside Spa
stops.body:
A regenerative permaculture farm threads between the pods — long contour beds, a circular mandala garden and herb spiral, a food forest, greenhouses and an irrigation pond — feeding residents and the wider network.

On the west pond, a Lakeside Spa: sauna, cold-plunge, soaking deck and treatment cabins on a low boardwalk.

stops.title: The Bigger Picture
stops.body:
Wells Gray is the flagship — a real, operating prototype and the blueprint for a network of aligned villages.

Explore the full vision, or get in touch about visiting, membership, or growing one on your own land.
